title: Lexical Retrieval with TF-IDF, BM25, Fields, and Exact Terms · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed retrieval review reconstructs lexical retrieval with tf-idf, bm25, fields, and exact terms from source, document, chunk, model, index, query, candidate, response, citation, evaluation, operations, and stakeholder evidence, then attacks one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for tested lexical retrieval baseline after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Compute term and document frequency from a declared token stream and explain saturation, repetition, and field effects.
Implement and document one TF-IDF formula, smoothing, sublinear term frequency, normalization, and library-specific convention.
Apply BM25 term saturation and length normalization with measured parameters rather than unexplained defaults.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose synonym, vocabulary mismatch, morphology, spelling, language, and semantic-intent failures using labeled query slices. Artifact under review: tested lexical retrieval baseline.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient Rag Lexical Baseline Failure query, omit corpus and version identity, relevance judgments, tenant policy, changed documents, abstention, citations, failure and recovery, then accept a plausible answer as RAG proof. This does not satisfy Rag Lexical Baseline Failure for tested lexical retrieval baseline.
The record names the assumption, applies Rag Lexical Baseline Failure, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Rag Lexical Baseline Failure record says tested lexical retrieval baseline looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Rag Lexical Baseline Failure.

steps.title: RAG and Python Record what required effort · Review Rag Lexical Baseline Failure
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Rag Lexical Baseline Failure: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review RAG engineering team handles case 9118 during a public-benefits policy change. Build deterministic Python evidence to compute inspectable TF-IDF or BM25-style lexical evidence and preserve exact identifiers; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Diagnose synonym, vocabulary mismatch, morphology, spelling, language, and semantic-intent failures using labeled query slices.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Record what required effort · Review Rag Lexical Baseline Failure.
steps.why: Retrieving Rag Lexical Baseline Failure through the concrete tested lexical retrieval baseline case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RAG and Python Record what required effort · Review Rag Lexical Baseline Failure, keep Rag Lexical Baseline Failure connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Diagnose synonym, vocabulary mismatch, morphology, spelling, language, and semantic-intent failures using labeled query slices.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Diagnose synonym, vocabulary mismatch, morphology, spelling, language, and semantic-intent failures using labeled query slices.

For RAG and Python Record what required effort · Review Rag Lexical Baseline Failure, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: One poor lexical example proves dense retrieval will be better for the whole query distribution.
